qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference on binary data download endpoint allows cross-user data access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns missing authorization on GET /rest/binary-data/, not stored XSS in the markdown rendering component used by workflow sticky notes or other markdown UI surfaces.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via workflow import-from-url endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unvalidated server-side fetching in GET /rest/workflows/from-url via axios.get(query.url), not markdown rendering or sanitizer bypass leading to stored XSS.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded multipart form data processing in webhook handler enables denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns missing multipart parser limits in the webhook form-data handler, not stored XSS through markdown rendering in workflow UI content.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The target advisory describes stored cross-site scripting in n8n's markdown rendering component used by workflow sticky notes and other markdown-capable UI content. The final qualifying findings instead cover an import-from-url SSRF, a binary-data authorization bypass, and webhook multipart resource exhaustion. None identifies the advisory-described markdown renderer, sticky-note/UI markdown path, sanitizer bypass, or stored XSS root cause.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-qpq4-pw7f-pp8w summary: n8n stored XSS via markdown rendering in workflow UI, including sticky notes.
ssrf-workflow-from-url reports GET /rest/workflows/from-url and axios.get(query.url) without URL destination validation.
idor-binary-data reports GET /rest/binary-data/ serving binary data without checking ownership.
webhook-multipart-dos reports formidable multipart parsing with no total file, field size, or field count limit.
Session output.scanned lists only idor-binary-data, ssrf-workflow-from-url, and webhook-multipart-dos as final qualifying_vuln folders.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause, so advisory-tier runtime evaluation is not applicable.
